summary.what: Add timbers doctor 'Binary Shadowing' check and clearer ack commit-block error for stale hook binaries
summary.why: The git hook runs whatever timbers is first on PATH; a stale binary (pre-v0.22.0) silently blocks commits by not recognizing acks — surfacing the divergence proactively beats debugging a confusing 'failed to commit ack file'
summary.how: doctor enumerates timbers binaries on PATH (deduped by resolved path), warns when the first reports a different version token than a shadowed one (skipping '?' tokens to avoid false positives on broken shims); WriteAck's commit-failure path now explains the staged-but-uncommitted state and points at upgrade + doctor + git commit recovery

notes: Discovered live during the v0.22.3 release: a 'dev' go-install binary in mise's GOBIN shadowed the current ~/.local/bin one, so 'timbers ack' wrote the record but its auto-commit was gate-blocked by the stale hook. Version token comparison (not full --version string) so two builds of the same version with different commit/date suffixes don't false-warn. Verified both pass (same version) and warn (fake older binary prepended to PATH) paths.
